channel.title: Blogs on Lalith Chandra
channel.description: Recent content in Blogs on Lalith Chandra

item.title: Surface diving into Tenstorrent's Wormhole architecture
item.description:
0: Overview and stats

This blog will be focusing mainly on it’s microarchitecture, along with their NoC. I will touch their peripherals and compiler a lil as well, but won’t be digging too much into their ISA, because honestly, it’s a documentation hell in their tt-metal and ISA documentation repo (not complaining tho, thankyou TT for making it open, very cool).

Before understanding their microarchitecture, it’s good to know the performance it delivers (LOL jk, I just put it there to sound like a nerd).
Below is a table comparing their various accelerator cards with their performance and hardware specifications. We’ll discuss everything soon dw.

item.title: Some cool stuff
item.description:
Links

  • HIP docs: https://rocm.docs.amd.com/projects/HIP/en/latest/
  • Khronos for HPC: https://www.khronos.org/sycl/
  • Hugh Perkins’ GPU: https://github.com/hughperkins/VeriGPU
  • NPU for LLMs: https://arxiv.org/pdf/2602.12962
  • Activation engine: https://arxiv.org/pdf/2512.23062
  • Accelerator for 1.58-bit LLM: https://arxiv.org/pdf/2510.15926 – 25 Tokens/s in 5W
  • VTR: https://github.com/verilog-to-routing/vtr-verilog-to-routing
  • ATMel: https://start.atmel.com/
  • HALO’s FRAME (opensource smartglasses): https://docs.brilliant.xyz/frame/frame/
  • FPGAs and DNNs: https://youtu.be/WWCWsub3YkE?si=vo1GEcfPHKgIRKZw
  • BORPH (FPGA-supportive OS): https://casper.berkeley.edu/wiki/BORPH
  • CASPERFPGA py lib: https://github.com/casper-astro/casperfpga

item.title: Rebellion via MDM Prison Break with Odin (Part I)
item.description:
This is the first part of my blog where I attempt to flash a new OS into a tablet, that has been carrying a firmware, locked down with heavy restrictions.

For context, I’ve got my hands on a government-provided Samsung tablet (won’t be disclosing model or the provider for paranoia-reasons) which has been “locked” for general usage, and allows only 8 applications that can be used (restricted using a third party MDM software), strictly for academic purposes (including instagram for whatever reasons?).

Timeloop Installation Troubleshooting Guide

Environment

  • OS: Debian Linux
  • Compiler: GCC 15
  • Build system: Make + SCons
  • Repo: accelergy-timeloop-infrastructure

Problem 1: Implicit Function Declaration Error in polylib

Error

./applications/testCompressParms.c:58:9: error: implicit declaration of function
'test_Constraints_Remove_parm_eqs'

Cause

Newer GCC versions treat implicit function declarations as errors.
The polylib test file calls undeclared functions.

Fix

Pass warning suppression flags to Make:

rm -rf /tmp/build-timeloop
make install_timeloop CFLAGS="-w -Wno-implicit-function-declaration" CXXFLAGS="-w"

Problem 2: Undefined Reference to glp_* Functions

Error

/usr/bin/x86_64-linux-gnu-ld.bfd: /usr/local/lib/libbarvinok.a(polysign_glpk.o):
in function `solve_lp': undefined reference to `glp_create_prob'

Cause

libbarvinok was compiled with GLPK support but glpk was not linked during the SCons build.
SCons ignores the LDFLAGS environment variable.
